summary: ai-sdk-helpers postinstall harvests developer identity (git config, gh CLI config, host/user info) and exfiltrates to a remote Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads installer-side files the package never wrote — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml — to extract the developer's git email, GitHub login, and GitHub email. It additionally collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), platform, and CI provider, then POSTs the resulting JSON identity payload to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. The in-file claim of 'fully anonymous' diagnostics is contradicted by the payload, which transmits PII tying the installer's machine to their GitHub identity. The destination is unrelated to the package's stated purpose (Vercel AI SDK helpers) and not affiliated with the Vercel AI SDK ecosystem the package name and description impersonate (author 'AI SDK Guide <hello@ai-sdk.guide>', homepage ai-sdk.guide — not Vercel). Reading ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (which also contains the user's GitHub oauth_token) at install time is unauthorized access to a credential-bearing file. The combination of namespace impersonation of '@ai-sdk/*' packages plus install-time identity harvesting is a deliberate supply-chain attack, not benign telemetry.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
